(b) Add a deterministic LAYOUT normalizer at assembly (same class as the existing `_materialize_paragraph_breaks` :3686-3692 and `markdown_table_normalizer` — these already ship): split any heading line that continues into prose at the heading/body boundary; enforce blank lines around headings. This is markdown layout normalization of writer-emitted text — zero word changes, zero content drops — the same category as the render steps the pipeline already performs, NOT a post-gen content edit. (c) Blueprint rule: per-section limitation prose is written into the section body (no repeated dedicated subheading role); the report-level Limitations section owns the register (one instance). WHY GENERAL: all three are format-level rules independent of task/domain. FAITHFULNESS: untouched (no sentence added/removed/changed).

**(5) SMALL TEST.** Deterministic lint on the emitted report (seconds, no LLM): zero heading lines containing sentence text after the title phrase (today: ≥2); zero repeated identical subheading titles beyond skeleton roles (today: 4-6× "Evidence Limitations"); zero mid-sentence-truncatable preamble (absent entirely); zero verbatim duplicate sentences ≥15 words (today ≥2). Then one paired 3v3 RACE probe read on Read; our sub-parity Read (.45-.50 vs ref .5) has the documented +0.045/dim-point at-parity leverage (SCORING_SPEC I.9 [F]).

### GAP-10 — Implications/future-agenda and balanced-impacts cells depend on corpus luck; the conclusion role doesn't demand them

**(1) GAP.** The skeleton's closing role (`:955-956`) asks for "CONCLUSIONS and open RESEARCH GAPS: what the evidence collectively supports, and what remains unresolved or under-studied" — it never asks the two winner-grade upgrades: (i) recommendations/implications tied to the diagnosed mechanisms with change-conditions, and (ii) gap statements converted to testable designs/observables. Our current conclusions do list gaps (7phase cleaned lines 207-213 — four generic gap bullets), but none states the observation that would resolve it; there is no balanced benefit/harm treatment obligation anywhere (Comp #6, 0.0290: mf_baseline has no section or focus clause owning "challenges AND opportunities").

**(2) SUB-ITEM LOST.** Insight #11 implications & future agendas (**0.0480**) + Comp #6 balanced impacts (0.0290).

**(3) WINNER MOVE.** Win-map #11: "each rec states mechanism-changed/population/trade-off/evidence-strength/testable-outcome; forward-looking must be grounded"; Pattern 11 "FORWARD-LOOKING BUT FALSIFIABLE — state what evidence is missing + what observation resolves it" (ref:300-307; NVIDIA:391-401; Lunon N5 falsifiers; Xiaoyi N14 per-section Critical-Evidence-Gap blocks); fable5's ":98 Threshold that would change this."

**(4) FIX (outliner skeleton role text; PRE-generation).** Strengthen the closing role's directive, generally: *"For each conclusion, name the mechanism or evidence family that supports it; for each open gap, state what data, comparison, or observation would resolve it; where the evidence shows both benefits and harms, state for whom and under what conditions each dominates."* WHY GENERAL: mechanism-attribution, resolvability, and distributional balance are properties of any evidence synthesis; no domain nouns, no counts. FAITHFULNESS: prose obligations over already-cited findings only.

**(5) SMALL TEST.** Deterministic: every gap bullet in the closing section contains a resolvability clause (pattern: names a study design/data/observation — lexicon-free check: clause references future evidence with a concrete object drawn from the report's own vocabulary); today 0/4 in 7phase. Plus the shared paired RACE probe (Insight read).

### GAP-11 (FACT) — E.Cit is a volume game we are losing 5–15× while precision is fine

**(1) GAP.** Measured (`results/fact/*/fact_result.txt`): b0fact E.Cit 16.0 @ 94.1% valid_rate; b1fact 31.0 @ 68.9%; faithoff_t72 11.0 @ 100%. The benchmark's FACT champion (gemini DR, the reference generator) posts **E.Cit 165.34 @ C.Acc 78.3** (TEARDOWN Part 4: "at near-equal precision, E.Cit is a 4× VOLUME game"). Structural cause in our artifacts: the current draw cites only 41 distinct references inline (183 markers; measured on raw 7phase_full_draw_1) and our sentences pack multiple facts per single `[N]` (the writer's rule 8 one-proposition-per-sentence helps, but the bibliography dedup + small admitted-work count caps distinct pairs).

**(2) SUB-ITEM LOST.** FACT E.Cit (surface #46: unique SUPPORTED pairs are linear; #48/#49 multiplicities) — an entire leaderboard column, orthogonal to RACE (SCORING_SPEC II).

**(3) WINNER MOVE.** TEARDOWN Part 4: many unique inline statement-URL pairs at maintained precision; per-section evidence-scoped writing (WebWeaver 25%→85.9%); AI-Q's per-research-unit citation contract.

**(4) FIX (retrieval + writer; PRE-generation).** The binding constraint is distinct admitted works reaching cited prose, not marker syntax (ours is already extractable: `[N]` ↔ URL-bearing References). Two general moves: (a) GAP-5's ledger-typed deepening directly raises distinct admitted works per facet (each new admitted work that yields ≥1 supported claim adds ≥1 pair, linearly); (b) writer rule already demands one proposition per sentence — add the general converse: *"when several admitted sources independently support distinct facts, write each fact as its own cited sentence rather than folding them into one summary sentence"* (this is surface #49 as a writing rule). WHY GENERAL: no counts, no domains; it scales E.Cit with real corpus abundance only. FAITHFULNESS: more atomic citation, never looser support.

**(5) SMALL TEST.** Extraction-and-dedup ONLY (no scrape/validate — cheap, deterministic-ish): run the FACT extract+dedup stages on a fixed report before/after; metric = unique statement-URL pairs (proxy upper bound of E.Cit). Today ≈41-111 distinct sources → tens of pairs; target: growth proportional to admitted-work growth from GAP-5's test. A follow-up full FACT run on the final candidate validates valid_rate ≥ baseline (no precision regression).

---

## PART B — Required flags

### B.1 Why our existing levers measured FLAT (wrong wiring vs wrong idea, per lever — grounded)

| lever | verdict | evidence |
|---|---|---|
| `contradiction_mining` | **wrong wiring** (idea right) | yields ZERO confirmed conflicts on the real corpus (`mf_max` draw_1 compose_summary `contradictions_detected: 0`) because the judge files level/method/period divergence — the very thing cell #8 pays for — as non_comparable/compatible (`contradiction_mining.py:123-126`) and `find_contradictions:167` discards those classes with their boundary `reason`s. Consumer is a hedging block, not a reconciliation obligation. Fix = GAP-3. |
| `relation_evidence_packs` | **wrong wiring** (idea right) | grouping key is a token-bag of the statement (`:56-66`) → pseudo-propositions; delivery is a raw JSON dump in the system prompt with one guidance sentence (`multi_section_generator.py:4188-4198`); global map reaches only the synthesis section. No comparative artifact or moderator obligation results. Fix = GAP-4. |
| `PG_SYNTHESIS_TABLE_CONSTRUCT` / `PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_COMPOSE` | **wrong idea for the cell it targets** | a `Finding|Value|Source` sentence-span inventory duplicates prose and is deterministically degraded by the citation-stripping cleaner (observed: malformed 2-col remnants + `Finding:` orphan lines in 7phase cleaned :129-157/:59-61). Winners' tables are shared-dimension matrices + interpretation. Retire/replace per GAP-4. |
| `PG_SECTION_STRUCTURE` | idea right, **unpoliced output** | produced run-in `### heading **prose**` lines (raw 7phase :33/:42) with no layout normalizer → Read losses ate the gains. Fix = GAP-9(b). |
| `PG_COVERAGE_OBLIGATIONS` | idea right, **toothless audit + wrong binding** | audit counts an obligation fulfilled iff its bound section is non-empty (`coverage_obligations.py:149-158`); binding put "4IR driver" on the CONCLUSION section (mf_max audit). No heading/echo requirement, no retrieval trigger. Fixes = GAP-5 + GAP-7. |
| `PG_COVERAGE_SPINE` / `PG_SCOPE_DEEPENING` | **inert in every measured run** | resolved_lever_states: both empty in mf_baseline/mf_max/7phase champion configs; deepening at this seam logs "requires_retrieval_pipeline" (`compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:431`). A lever that never runs measures exactly 0. Fix = wire per GAP-5(d). |
| `contradiction_hedging`, `PG_NARRATIVE_ATTRIBUTION` | idea right, **advisory-only delivery** | hedging asks to "acknowledge" disagreements (not explain); attribution metadata rides as a sidecar but no rule compels venue-in-prose at first mention (GAP-8(b)(ii)). |

### B.2 Gaps NOT fixable pre-generation (and why)

1. **Cleaner nondeterminism/truncation** (SCORING_SPEC I.10): the judged text is an LLM rewrite we don't control — our preamble was truncated mid-sentence and tables were mangled in-flight. We can only make artifacts robust-to-cleaning (GAP-4/GAP-9 do); we cannot fix the cleaner. Residual risk stays.
2. **Single-call judge variance** (±0.027; SCORING_SPEC Part V #7): irreducible in-pipeline; every test above uses deterministic structural assertions first and paired multi-draw probes second.
3. **Reference contamination and the relative frame** (SCORING_SPEC I.11): a target stylistically close to the Gemini reference benefits; not an actionable pre-gen lever for us beyond adopting the winning surfaces themselves.
4. **NVIDIA-style post-gen rubric rewrite** is the one competitor move that is structurally forbidden for us (no-post-gen rule); its 10 editor instructions are instead absorbed into GAP-1/2/4/6/10's writer- and outline-stage rules — which is exactly the brief's demand.
5. **Two-column FACT reality**: E.Cit depends on TODAY's page reachability at scoring time (Jina drift, SCORING_SPEC II.3) — not controllable pre-generation beyond choosing stable URLs at retrieval.

---

## PART C — PRIORITIZED FIX LIST (highest effective-weight × our-headroom first)

1. **GAP-1 (Insight #7+#8, 0.0800+0.0800; headroom ~5-7 leaderboard pts).** One-line fix: add a licensed-inference writer rule — every ≥3-sentence evidence paragraph closes with ONE inference sentence deriving what its already-cited findings jointly imply, introducing zero new facts/numbers and carrying the paragraph's own markers. Small test: deterministic `paragraph_deduction_rate` (0.16 → >0.6) + zero-new-number canary, then a 3v3 paired same-judge RACE probe on Insight.
2. **GAP-3 (Insight #8, 0.0800).** One-line fix: harvest ALL contradiction-judge verdict classes (conflict/compatible/non_comparable + boundary reasons) into a divergence ledger that obligates the synthesis section to EXPLAIN each divergence by its recorded boundary (population/method/level/period/measure). Small test: miner unit-run on the frozen corpus — ledger non-empty where today's yield is a measured 0 — plus a deterministic "synthesis names ≥1 boundary term per covered pair" assertion and the paired probe.
3. **GAP-2 (Insight #7, 0.0800 + #9 0.0480).** One-line fix: add a fifth, question-type-conditional skeleton role — an explanatory-framework section placed before the evidence bodies, whose channels every body section's focus must reference. Small test: outline-JSON assertion (framework present ≤ position 2; ≥50% of body foci name a channel) at 1 outline call, then the paired probe.
4. **GAP-5 (Comp #2+#3, 0.0725+0.0725 + Inst #16 0.0375).** One-line fix: build a question-derived coverage ledger (named dimensions + entity-class quantifiers + the four analytical roles), let the checklist ground gaps in it, type deepening queries per row (factual/causal/comparative/critical), and turn deepening ON at this seam. Small test: retrieval-only ledger-fulfillment telemetry (distinct entity-class instances with evidence: 3 → reference-class 8+) + outline ownership assertion, then a Comp-read paired probe.
5. **GAP-4 (Insight #8 0.0800 shared + Read #22/#23/#24 0.0560).** One-line fix: retire the sentence-span `Finding|Value|Source` tables from the judged report; regroup relation packs by shared-measure × different-context using the pack's existing attribute schema and have the WRITER emit the comparison (prose or valid table) followed by a mandatory interpretation paragraph. Small test: simulated-clean lint (0 malformed tables, 0 duplicate sentences; today 8 malformed lines + ≥2 dupes) + multi-context comparative-sentence count, then the paired probe.
6. **GAP-6 (Insight #10, 0.0640).** One-line fix: synthesis-role directive to derive NAMED cross-cutting propositions grounded in ≥2 cited findings, each labeled with a fixed three-level evidential-status vocabulary and, when not established, the test that would resolve it. Small test: deterministic labeled-proposition count (0 → ≥1 per multi-member cluster) + paired probe on Insight.
7. **GAP-8 (Inst #17+#18, 0.0625 + Comp #5 0.0435).** One-line fix: default-on admission eligibility when an exclusive source constraint is extracted, telemetry-derived in-prose admission-policy sentence, venue-at-first-mention from existing evidence metadata, and reader-vocabulary-only Limitations (no tier codes). Small test: three deterministic lints (zero internal-vocab tokens — today FAILS; policy-sentence ⊆ telemetry; venue-attribution rate ~0 → ≳0.8) + Inst-read paired probe.
8. **GAP-7 (Inst #13-#16, 0.1625 combined band; cheap credit).** One-line fix: outline rule — every question-named topic/scope term owns a heading in the question's own words, and the title names the required genre. Small test: deterministic heading-coverage lint against extracted constraint phrases (today ≥2 named topics unmatched) at one outline call.
9. **GAP-9 (Read #20/#21/#22/#24, 0.0840 combined; we are BELOW parity where winners are above).** One-line fix: drop the hardcoded driver preamble, add a deterministic layout normalizer (split run-in headings; blank-line hygiene), and make per-section limitation prose non-templated (one report-level Limitations role). Small test: four-assert render lint (run-in headings ≥2→0; repeated subheads 4-6→0; preamble gone; verbatim dupes ≥2→0), then Read-read paired probe.
10. **GAP-10 (Insight #11 0.0480 + Comp #6 0.0290).** One-line fix: closing-role directive — each conclusion names its supporting mechanism/evidence family, each gap states its resolving observation, and benefit/harm balance states for whom and when. Small test: resolvability-clause presence per gap bullet (0/4 → 4/4) + shared paired probe.
11. **GAP-11 (FACT E.Cit; separate track).** One-line fix: scale distinct supported pairs by (a) ledger-typed deepening (more admitted works) and (b) a one-fact-one-cited-sentence writer rule for independently supported facts. Small test: FACT extract+dedup-only pair count on fixed reports before/after (16-31 supported today vs champion 165), full FACT run on the final candidate to confirm valid_rate holds.

**Measurement discipline for ALL of the above** (SCORING_SPEC Part V #7 + measured baseline spread 0.4922–0.5088): each fix ships with its deterministic assertion FIRST (free, noise-immune), and score attribution uses paired same-judge multi-draw probes (≥3v3, same corpus, same config otherwise), judged on the paired mean per-dimension delta — never a single-draw comparison. Build-all-then-measure applies: the deterministic tests gate each lever individually; the RACE probe measures the assembled set.

content.text: All ten artifacts read in full. Now I'll extract the exact receipt lines (FIRST / MID / LAST verbatim) for the ingestion receipt.

input.command:
cd /home/polaris/wt/faithoff/docs/race_fact_initiative
for spec in "SCORING_SPEC.md 123" "phase1_sol_verdict.md 231" "phase1_fable_verdict.md 236" "COMPETITOR_TEARDOWN.md 212" "phase2_sol_verdict.md 574" "phase2_fable_verdict.md 136" "phase2_addendum_fable_verdict.md 168" "PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md 410" "phase3_sol_verdict.md 134" "phase3_fable_verdict.md 110"; do
  f=$(echo $spec | cut -d' ' -f1); m=$(echo $spec | cut -d' ' -f2)
  n=$(wc -l < "$f")
  first=$(grep -m1 -n '[^[:space:]]' "$f" | head -1)
  last=$(grep -n '[^[:space:]]' "$f" | tail -1)
  mid=$(sed -n "${m}p" "$f")
  echo "=== $f | lines=$n"
  echo "FIRST: $first"
  echo "MID($m): $mid"
  echo "LAST: $last"
  echo
done

## 0. Scope and method

Design-only; no pipeline file touched. Every rule and wave below is grounded in the ten artifacts above by
file:line. The build list is exactly the fourteen unified gaps U1–U14 (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:79-664`), the wiring
facts of `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:57-76`, the flat-lever forensics of `:668-679`, and the Phase-4 architectural
thesis both investigators converged on independently (`:810-820`; `phase3_sol_verdict.md:267`;
`phase3_fable_verdict.md:220`). Measured ground truth used throughout: champion = **mf_baseline** (all levers OFF
except `PG_RENDER_BLOCKS=1`, strict-verify bypassed) at Overall mean **0.5009**, draw spread 0.4922–0.5088
(`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:43-55`; `phase3_fable_verdict.md:36-42`); noise bar **±0.027** per
`SCORING_SPEC.md:245`; the two 0.0800 Insight cells are the largest scorecard cells (`SCORING_SPEC.md I.7:121`).

# DELIVERABLE A — EXECUTION_CHARTER

Five gating rules. Every fix, in every wave, must pass ALL FIVE before it ships. Each rule states (i) the
enforceable rule, (ii) how a reviewer AUDITS it mechanically, (iii) the stop condition.

## R1 — GENERALIZATION GATE (no task-72 overfit ships)

**Rule.** A fix is admissible only if ALL of the following hold:

1. **Zero task/domain literals** in code, prompts, and config it introduces. Banned classes: task-72 domain nouns
   (e.g. labor/industry/4IR/journal-name strings), benchmark-task identifiers, venue whitelists, and any
   domain-vocabulary lookup table. The only FIXED vocabularies permitted are the audited **role/type/status
   vocabularies**, which are properties of research questions and evidence, not domains
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:306-316`): analytical roles {mechanism, cross-context comparison,
   consensus/disagreement, implications}; query types {factual, causal, comparative, critical}; comparability
   boundaries {population, method, level, period, measure} (the same fields
   `relation_evidence_packs._attributes` already aliases, `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:210-212`); the three-level
   epistemic-status vocabulary (`:381-389`). CONTENT always comes from the question + admitted evidence at run
   time.
2. **Zero magic counts** (no hardcoded section counts, industry counts, word targets) and **zero adjective flag
   names** (no `PG_BETTER_X`-style levers; components are named by the seam/object they build).
3. **Held-out proof.** The fix's deterministic small test (the audit's SMALL TEST for that gap) passes on
   task 72 AND on **≥2 unrelated held-out benchmark tasks** with materially different weight profiles.
   Named candidates from the frozen criteria distribution (`SCORING_SPEC.md I.5:74-77`):
   - **task 91** (Saint Seiya inventory; weights .37 Comp / **.11 Insight** / .32 Inst / .20 Read) — the
     anti-analytical extreme; conditional analytical triggers (U2 mechanism role, U5 analytical-role ledger rows)
     must NOT fire or must degrade gracefully;
   - **task 100** (AI & interpersonal relationships; .29/.40/.16/.15) — the high-Insight analytical extreme;
   - **task 73** (novice EFL teachers; Read **.25**, highest in corpus) — audience/readability stressor;
   - **task 51** (Japan elderly consumption; data-heavy, heading-sparse reference) — data-presentation stressor;
   - **task 4** (zh, gold + mind-map deliverable; .20/.38/.26/.16) — language + explicit format-instruction
     stressor.
   Mandatory pair: **91 + 100** (opposite Insight extremes). Recommended add: 73 or 51. Operator picks the final
   set (Open Question OQ-1).

**Audit procedure.** (a) `grep` the diff for the banned classes: any token that appears in the task-72 prompt or
criteria but not in the fixed role/type/status vocabularies fails review; reviewer greps for digits in prompt/
config additions to catch magic counts. (b) Run the fix's deterministic test on the held-out tasks' frozen
corpora/outlines; the trigger-conditionality check on task 91 is mandatory for any analytically-conditioned
component. (c) Confirm the component reads its content from the constraint extractor
(`compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:398-410`) or admitted evidence rows, never from its own literals.

**Stop.** Any literal/magic-count/adjective-name finding, or a held-out deterministic-test failure, blocks the
wave until fixed. No exceptions for "temporary" literals.

## R2 — FAITHFULNESS FIREWALL (untouchable set + licensed inference + no post-gen edits)

**Rule.**
1. **Untouchable files.** No fix modifies `provenance_generator.py` (rewrite + citation resolution,
   `:3714-3943`, `:4637-5252`) or `clinical_generator/strict_verify.py` (identifier/span/numeric/overlap checks,
   `:387-574`). These are claim-admission controls, not score planners (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:69-73`). No new
   entailment/NLI machinery is introduced anywhere (standing operating rule; the champion runs strict-verify OFF
   and the initiative's fixes must be valid under both strict-verify configurations).
2. **Every sentence still carries evidence markers.** All new writer obligations produce sentences that carry
   `[ev_XXX]` markers exactly as today (`multi_section_generator.py:3517-3519`).
3. **Licensed-inference admission check (the U1 canary).** The ONE new sentence class — the paragraph-closing
   inference — is admitted only under the deterministic **zero-new-factual-token canary**: the closing sentence
   introduces no numeric token, no new proper-noun/entity, and no factual claim absent from the preceding cited
   sentences of that same paragraph, and carries the union of that paragraph's markers
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:111-128`). The same canary gates U7's labeled propositions (each grounded in ≥2 cited
   findings; the label explicitly marks the report's own inference as inference — the faithful way to be novel,
   `:381-389`) and U10's implication objects (derived only from upstream cited propositions, `:469-476`).
4. **NO post-generation content edit, EVER.** No sentence is added, removed, or reworded after composition.
   The NVIDIA-style rubric rewrite is the one competitor move structurally forbidden to us
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:727-729`); its surfaces are absorbed pre-generation into U1/U2/U4/U7/U10.
   **Sole labeled exception — layout-only render normalization**: deterministic markdown layout operations on
   writer-emitted text with ZERO word changes and ZERO content drops (split run-in heading lines at the
   heading/body boundary, enforce blank lines around headings), the same category as the shipping
   `_materialize_paragraph_breaks` (`multi_section_generator.py:3686-3692`) and `markdown_table_normalizer`
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:578-583`). Any operation that touches a word, a number, a marker, or sentence
   membership is a content edit and is banned. The normalizer must ship with a diff-auditor asserting
   token-multiset equality before/after (layout characters excluded).

**Audit procedure.** (a) `git diff --stat` of the wave must show zero lines changed in the untouchable files.
(b) The canary runs as a deterministic lint on every composed artifact (it is Wave 1's shipped test).
(c) The render normalizer's token-multiset equality check runs on every assembly.
(d) Reviewer greps new prompts for instructions that would license out-of-evidence content beyond the one
inference class.

**Stop.** Any untouchable-file diff, any canary failure, any normalizer token change → the wave does not ship.
If strict verification removes a planned sentence, the lawful response is stronger evidence ownership or
regeneration from the plan, never weakening faithfulness or editing the finished report
(`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:724-726`).

## R3 — MEASUREMENT GATE (deterministic first, paired probe second, stop rule)

**Rule.** Two-tier proof, in order, per `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:802-806`:
1. **Tier 1 — deterministic structural assertion FIRST.** Every component ships its audit-named SMALL TEST as a
   zero/low-LLM deterministic assertion (free, noise-immune). A component whose deterministic test fails does not
   proceed to any RACE spend. Concrete gates per wave in §B3.
2. **Tier 2 — paired same-judge multi-draw RACE probe.** ≥3 candidate draws vs ≥3 champion draws (**3v3
   minimum**), same corpus, same judge, otherwise-identical config, decided on the **paired MEAN per-dimension
   delta clearing ±0.027** (the measured baseline spread 0.4922–0.5088 IS this bar,
   `phase3_fable_verdict.md:40`). Never a single-draw comparison.
3. **Build-all-then-measure.** Deterministic tests gate each component individually; the RACE probe measures the
   assembled wave (and, at the end, the assembled ladder), per the standing build-all-then-measure order and
   `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:805-806`. Within-wave components are not RACE-probed one at a time.
4. **STOP RULE.** A component whose deterministic test fails does not ship. A wave whose paired probe shows any
   dimension's paired mean regressing beyond −0.027 does not ship as-is: the wave is decomposed (components
   staged off via the contract's component switches, §R4), the offending component identified by its
   deterministic telemetry, and either repaired or retired. A wave that is flat (all paired means inside ±0.027,
   none negative beyond it) MAY ship only if its deterministic gates all pass AND it is a declared dependency of
   a later wave (foundation shipping), with the flat result recorded — never spun as a win.

**Audit procedure.** Probe manifests record judge model, corpus hash, config hash, draw IDs; reviewer recomputes
the paired means from `results/race/*/raw_results.jsonl`. Any probe missing its paired champion arm is invalid.

**Stop.** As in rule 4 above; additionally, any probe run against a non-champion comparator is void (see R5).

## R4 — THE SHARED-CONTRACT RULE (one contract, consumed by the active producer, audited semantically)

**Rule.** Both Phase-3 investigators converged independently (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:24-28`, `:810-820`): the fix
is **ONE pre-generation analytical contract** — hereafter the **Analytical Contract (AC)** — not a set of
independent flags. Therefore:
1. **One object.** The AC is a single per-run artifact holding the components the waves build: coverage-dimension
   ledger (U5), mechanism/causal-chain spine (U2), divergence ledger + relation graph (U3), comparison groups /
   table plans (U4/U6), epistemically-labeled inference plans (U7), concept-role spine (U8), measurement/effect
   ontology (U9), implication objects (U10), source-admission ledger (U11), block/layout plan (U12), routing
   ownership (U13), external-citation claim contract (U14).
2. **One consumption point.** Every component is consumed by the **active producer,
   `_compose_section_per_basket`** — the verified-compose primary branch — not only by `_call_section`, which is
   the `else` branch (`multi_section_generator.py:6630-6728`; `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:58-61`). A prompt addendum
   routed only to `_call_section` misses the producer that emits the sentences; this exact miswiring is a proven
   cause of the measured-flat lever set (`:668-679`). Definition of done for every wave includes a wiring
   assertion that the component's payload is observable in the active producer's inputs.
3. **Semantic audit.** Fulfillment of any AC obligation is SEMANTIC: the planned/emitted proposition must ENTAIL
   the obligation (dimension-role pair, channel reference, boundary term, label, policy clause), never "the bound
   section is non-empty" — the toothless-audit failure of `coverage_obligations.py:139-158`
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:277-283`). ("Semantic" here means checked at proposition level by the audit's
   deterministic tests and fixtures — NOT an NLI/entailment model; R2 bans those.)
4. **No independent flags.** New behavior enters as an AC component with a component switch used ONLY for staged
   measurement (R3's decomposition) and rollback; the switch namespace is the AC's, not a new `PG_*` adjective
   flag. The independent-flag path is what measured flat (`:24-28`, `:818`).
5. **Advisory prose is not a fix.** A wave that only adds writer-prompt sentences without changing evidence
   ownership or plan objects must justify why it will not repeat the measured-flat failure — U1 qualifies because
   it changes the writer's licensed sentence CLASS and ships a deterministic behavioral gate, not because it is
   prose.

**Audit procedure.** (a) Wiring assertion: dump the active producer's assembled inputs on a fixture run and
assert the component payload is present (Sol's U2 test pattern, `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:164-170`). (b) Grep for
new top-level env flags outside the AC namespace. (c) Each obligation's fulfillment test is proposition-level.

**Stop.** A component not consumed by `_compose_section_per_basket`, or audited only by section-nonemptiness,
does not ship.

## R5 — NO-REGRESSION GATE (the champion is the only comparator, and it is protected)

**Rule.**
1. **The comparator is mf_baseline** — all levers OFF except `PG_RENDER_BLOCKS=1`, strict-verify bypassed as
   measured — at Overall mean **0.5009** (draws 0.5088/0.5017/0.4922; `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:46`, `:63-68`;
   `phase3_fable_verdict.md:42`). Every wave's Tier-2 probe runs candidate-vs-champion, paired, same judge, same
   corpus. Measuring against `mf_max`, `7phase`, `faithoff_t72`, or any other strawman is void — the champion is
   the HIGHEST measured config; beating a weaker arm proves nothing.
2. **Ship criterion.** A wave ships only if (a) paired mean Overall ≥ champion within noise (no Overall
   regression beyond −0.027), (b) no dimension regresses beyond −0.027 paired mean, and (c) its claimed win —
   if a win is claimed — clears +0.027 on the targeted dimension(s).
3. **Champion protection.** (i) The champion config (`resolved_lever_states` snapshot,
   `outputs/race_max_focus/mf_baseline-20260723T152731Z/draw_3/compose_summary.json`) is pinned in VCS as a named
   frozen config; (ii) its three baseline draws and their scores are retained as the reference distribution;
   (iii) the champion path stays runnable at all times — AC components default OFF until their wave ships, so
   the shipped pipeline with an empty AC must reproduce the champion behavior byte-for-byte at the config level;
   (iv) after each shipped wave, the new assembled config becomes the NEW champion for the next wave's probes
   (ladder discipline), with its own ≥3-draw reference set.
4. **Corpus/judge pinning.** Probes use the frozen corpus and record the judge model; cross-judge comparisons are
   forbidden (`SCORING_SPEC.md:219-234`: regimes not comparable).

**Audit procedure.** Probe manifest must name the champion config hash and cite its reference draws; an
empty-AC run is diffed against the pinned champion config before any wave's probe is accepted.

**Stop.** Any probe without the champion arm, or any shipped wave that cannot reproduce the champion under
empty-AC, is reverted.

---

# DELIVERABLE B — MASTER_ACTION_PLAN

## B1. BUILD WAVES (grouped by shared seam + dependency; each wave = one coherent contract extension)

Legend: gaps landed · shared AC object built/extended · exact files:functions (from the audit's evidence) ·
pre-gen seam · coherence rationale. All waves obey R1–R5.

### WAVE 1 — "Writer contract + render floor" (U1 + U12-lite + U14b) — the first-ship wave
- **Gaps landed:** U1 (licensed paragraph-closing inference — Insight #7+#8, 0.0800+0.0800); U12-lite (layout
  normalizer, hardcoded-preamble removal, single Limitations role — Read #19/#20/#21/#22/#24, 0.0840 combined,
  our BELOW-parity dim); U14b (one-fact-one-cited-sentence converse rule — FACT #49, rides free on the same
  prompt seam).
- **AC object built:** the **AC carrier itself** (empty-safe, champion-preserving) + its single consumption
  point inside `_compose_section_per_basket`, with the licensed-inference sentence class and the render/layout
  plan as its first two payloads. This satisfies R4 from day one: later waves extend this object; no wave adds a
  flag.
- **Files:functions:** `multi_section_generator.py:3517-3519` (SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE CRITICAL RULES —
  add the inference class), `:4420-4432` (retry contract — same), `:6630-6728` (`_compose_section_per_basket` —
  AC consumption), `:3686-3692` (`_materialize_paragraph_breaks` — the class the new normalizer joins),
  `:3581-3585` (own-line heading rule the normalizer enforces); `compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:680-694`
  (delete hardcoded preamble from the judged report); `multi_section_generator.py:8843-8917`
  (`_deterministic_reader_limitations` — pin reader-register, one report-level Limitations role);
  `cleaned_output_guard.py` (extend lints). (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:86-128`, `:546-592`, `:649-658`.)
- **Pre-gen seam:** writer prompt contract + deterministic assembly (layout-only exception, labeled per R2.4).
- **Why coherent:** one seam (what the writer is licensed/obligated to emit + how emitted text is laid out);
  zero upstream dependencies; contains both investigators' #1-ranked fix (U1,
  `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:742-745`) and the floor-repair for our only below-parity dimension (`:563-566`). The
  Bodhi labeled-deduction form is writer-chosen, not hardcoded (`:118-120`).

## B2. DEPENDENCY GRAPH + CRITICAL PATH

```
W1 U1  (writer inference class)  ──────────────┐   [no upstream deps]
W1 U12-lite (layout normalizer)  ──────────────┤   [no upstream deps]
W1 U14b (one-fact-one-sentence)  ──────────────┤
                                               ▼
W2 U3 (divergence ledger/graph) ──► W2 U4 (comparison groups/tables)
        │                                 └──► W2 U6 (regime schema)
        ├────────────────────────────► W5 U7-full (inference planner + labels)
        ├────────────────────────────► W4 U10-full (implication objects)
        └────────────────────────────► U14 dedup-identity planning (W3)
W3 U5 (coverage ledger + deepening) ──► W2/W5 U6 (instances to compare)
        ├────────────────────────────► W3 U14a (E.Cit volume: more admitted works)
        └────────────────────────────► W5 U13 (ownership needs the ledger)
W3 U11 (admission contract) ─────────► W3 U14a (URLs/quality of admitted works)
W4 U2 (mechanism spine) ─────────────► W1 U1's deductions get channels to reuse (enhances, not required)
        └────────────────────────────► W4 U10-full (implications point at diagnosed levers)
W4 U8 (concept-role spine) ── independent of U2 but same seam
W5 U9 (ontology) ── consumes W2 attributes; disciplines U4/U6 comparisons
W5 U12-full (block plan) ── consumes producer path; hardens everything for render
```

Notes: U1 does NOT require U2 — the audit's before-number (deduction rate 0.16 on mf_baseline) and fable5's
0.5131 Insight at 3,071 words prove the per-paragraph deduction pays standalone
(`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:101-105`); U2 later upgrades those deductions from paragraph-local to spine-reusing.
U6 sits across W2/W3: its schema mechanism is W2's, its instance supply is W3's; its full paired probe waits for
both.

**Critical path (score-weighted):** **U3 → U4/U6 → U7-full** — this chain carries Insight #8 (0.0800), Comp #3
(0.0725), and Insight #10 (0.0640), and its head (U3) converts a measured-zero lever into the substrate for
three later components. Secondary path: **U5 → U6/U13/U14a** (Comp breadth + FACT volume). U1 is off-path
(no dependents block on it) — which is exactly why it ships first: maximum expected value, zero blocking risk.

## B3. PER-WAVE GO/NO-GO (deterministic gates → expected dimensions → probe decision; cheapest pre-60-min test)

**Wave 1.**
- Deterministic gates: (a) `paragraph_deduction_rate` on composed sections: 0.16 (mf_baseline_draw_1) → **>0.6**
  (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:122-125`); (b) zero-new-factual-token canary: **0 violations** (R2.3); (c) render lint:
  run-in heading lines ≥2→**0**, repeated identical subheadings 4–6×→**0**, preamble **absent**, verbatim
  duplicate sentences ≥15 words ≥2→**0** (`:586-592`); (d) normalizer token-multiset equality: **exact**;
  (e) wiring assertion: inference directive present in `_compose_section_per_basket` inputs.
- Cheapest pre-60-min test: **compose 2–3 sections from frozen baskets through the active producer** (minutes,
  no retrieval, no full report) and run gates (a)–(e); the render lint runs on existing artifacts for free.
- Expected dimensions: **Insight ↑** (the two 0.0800 cells), **Read ↑** (0.0840 band, below parity → documented
  +0.045/dim-point at-parity leverage, `:591-592`).
- Probe decision: 3v3 paired vs champion; ship if Insight paired mean > +0.027 OR (Insight ≥ 0 AND Read > +0.027),
  AND no dimension < −0.027. The Read guard doubles as the U1-density risk check (§B5 RC-1).

**Wave 2.**
- Deterministic gates: (a) miner unit-run on the frozen corpus: divergence ledger **non-empty** with boundary
  reasons where today's harvest is a measured **0** (`:214-219`) — reuses already-paid judge verdicts, near-zero
  cost; (b) simulated-clean lint: strip citation columns then `find_malformed_tables` → **0** defects (today 8
  malformed lines) and **0** verbatim body-duplications (today ≥2) (`:264-270`); (c) comparative-coverage
  counter: sentences citing ≥2 rows with differing context labels present in every section owning a
  multi-context group (`:268-269`); (d) synthesis section names ≥1 recorded boundary term per covered pair;
  (e) U6 fixture: synonym contexts do NOT satisfy diversity; common-schema plan contains only shared dimensions
  (`:349-353`); (f) Sol's three-relation fixture: conflict / compatible-at-different-margins / unrelated produce

- Cheapest pre-60-min test: (a) is the wave's proof-of-life — an isolated miner re-harvest on stored verdicts

## B4. SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION

**Recommended build order: W1 → W2 → W3 → W4 → W5**, with the ladder discipline of R5.3 (each shipped wave
becomes the next comparator).

**First-ship wave: WAVE 1.** Rationale against the audit's own ranking (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:742-750`):
1. U1 is both investigators' independent #1 (proxy 0.04408 per 0.0800 cell, both cells at once); it needs no
   upstream object, its deterministic test is zero-LLM, and the behavior it targets is the single biggest
   winner-vs-midscorer difference on the board AND locally (fable5 0.5131 Insight vs champ 0.3411,
   `:101-105`).
2. **U1 and U3 are the right first PLAYS but not one wave**: they live in different seams (writer contract vs
   relation layer). Coupling them would violate wave coherence and make the paired probe unattributable. U3
   leads Wave 2 immediately after.
3. **No foundational contract wave needs to precede U1** — provided Wave 1's definition-of-done includes creating
   the AC carrier and its consumption point in `_compose_section_per_basket` (R4.1–R4.2). The contract-first
   requirement is satisfied by making the carrier part of Wave 1, with U1 as its first payload. A carrier-only
   "Wave 0" would ship nothing measurable and burn a probe cycle.
4. U12-lite rides along because it is free (deterministic, layout-only), repairs our only below-parity dimension,
   and its lints become the permanent render gate every later wave reuses. It also hedges U1's known Read-density
   risk in the same probe.
5. The smallest high-confidence first win is therefore exactly this wave: two 0.0800 cells + the 0.0840 Read
   band, gated by five deterministic asserts, provable on frozen baskets in minutes before any 60-minute
   generation.

Weight×headroom justification for the middle order: W2 carries #8+#5+#22-24 and converts a measured-zero lever
(cheapest evidence-to-value conversion in the plan); W3 is the only wave that changes CORPUS breadth (Comp
headroom .49→.55+ is the largest dimension-level headroom, `:323`) and unlocks U6/U13/U14; W4's outline roles
want the post-deepening corpus; W5 governs everything and closes #10.

## B5. RISK REGISTER

**A. Not-fixable-pre-generation (live-with items, from `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:715-733`):**
1. **Cleaner nondeterminism/truncation** — judged text is an LLM rewrite we don't control. Plan posture: make
   artifacts robust-to-cleaning (U4's simulated-clean lint, U12's layout hygiene); accept residual risk;
   never claim a cleaning-dependent win from one draw.
2. **Single-call judge variance ±0.027** — irreducible; R3's two-tier design exists because of it; all decisions
   on paired means.
3. **Reference frame + contamination** — RACE is relative to the frozen Gemini reference; not a lever beyond
   adopting the winning surfaces; never compare across judge regimes.
4. **FACT URL reachability / Jina drift** — E.Cit judged against TODAY's pages; posture: canonical stable URLs
   at retrieval (U14 preflight), accept the rest.
5. **Strict-verify sentence removal** — lawful response is stronger evidence ownership or plan-regeneration,
   never firewall weakening (R2).
6. **Post-gen rewrite forbidden** — the NVIDIA move stays absorbed into pre-gen rules; no exception creep beyond
   R2.4's labeled layout normalization.

**B. Fixes that RISK a different cell (cost cells + mitigations):**
- **RC-1 (U1 → Read #19/#22).** Per-paragraph deductions raise analytic density; the #1/#3/#4 leaderboard systems
  all bottom out on Read (cellcog 51.94, Bodhi 51.87, Lunon 50.48 — addendum F2, `COMPETITOR_TEARDOWN.md:399-401`).
  We start BELOW parity so we cannot afford the winners' trade. Mitigation: the rule licenses exactly ONE closing
  inference sentence per paragraph (structural cap, not a count knob); U12-lite ships in the same wave; Wave 1's
  probe reads Read as a co-primary guard.
- **RC-2 (U11 → Comp #2/#3/#5 and FACT E.Cit).** Eligibility enforcement shrinks the corpus (tier compliance
  ceiling ~73% measured, `:495-497`); Sourcery proves a thin sector base forfeits ~3 Comp points
  (`:293-295`). Mitigation: U5's typed deepening refills to the ledger's satisfaction BEFORE enforcement
  verdicts are final; unknown-eligibility rows become retrieval targets, not silent drops; Wave 3's gates couple
  ledger fulfillment WITH compliance so neither ships alone; residual shortfall is disclosed Dalpha/Bodhi-style
  (the honest scope note itself performs the constraint, `COMPETITOR_TEARDOWN.md:71-73`). Operator decision OQ-3
  governs the hard-enforce vs enforce-and-disclose balance.
- **RC-3 (U5 deepening → Inst #13 focus + Read walls).** More admitted evidence historically became more prose
  (route-all, 433-ID residual dump, `:599-604`). Mitigation: W3 ships ledger-SCOPED admission only; U13's
  marginal-contribution routing lands in W5 — until then, W3's gate includes "no residual-section role" as a
  regression lint (it is free) so the dump cannot silently return.
- **RC-4 (U4 tables → Read #23/#24 under the cleaner).** Any reintroduced table can degrade in-flight
  (`:717-719`). Mitigation: tables only from table PLANS with writer-emitted short-phrase cells under the
  existing rule (`:3595-3600`), always followed by the interpretation paragraph; the simulated-clean lint is a
  permanent gate; where comparability fails, plan prose (fable5 won Read 0.5262 WITH 14 table rows — well-formed
  tables are not the enemy, malformed ones are).
- **RC-5 (U7 labels → fabrication-adjacent risk).** A "this review's own hypothesis" label on an ungrounded claim
  would be unfaithful novelty. Mitigation: labels REQUIRE ≥2 cited premises + the R2.3 canary; the confounded
  fixture must yield "unresolved", not a hypothesis; unlabeled novelty remains banned.
- **RC-6 (U2 on non-analytical tasks → Inst/Comp).** A mechanism section on an inventory task (t91, Insight .11)
  would misallocate real estate. Mitigation: question-type conditionality via the constraint extractor + the
  mandatory t91 held-out no-fire test (R1.3).
- **RC-7 (U14 atomic cited sentences → Read #22 serial-summary).** One-fact-one-sentence can drift toward
  staccato inventory. Mitigation: the rule applies only to independently supported DISTINCT facts; paragraph
  organization stays claim-led (U1's closing inference gives each paragraph its synthesis); Read guard in every
  probe.
- **RC-8 (probe budget/schedule).** 5 waves × ≥3v3 draws ≈ 30+ full generations + judge calls; under-budgeting
  invites single-draw shortcuts, which R3 forbids. Mitigation: OQ-5 fixes the budget up front; deterministic
  gates kill weak components before any RACE spend.

## B6. OPEN QUESTIONS FOR THE OPERATOR (decisions blocking the build)

- **OQ-1 — Held-out set.** Confirm the generalization pair {task 91, task 100} + one of {73, 51, 4} (R1.3), and
  whether held-out runs are outline/compose-level only (cheap) or full-report (costly, more faithful).
- **OQ-2 — Retirements.** Confirm permanent retirement of `PG_SYNTHESIS_TABLE_CONSTRUCT` /
  `PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_COMPOSE` (wrong idea + post-gen violation) and the raw relation-pack JSON dump, vs
  keep-behind-AC-switch for forensics.
- **OQ-3 — Eligibility posture.** Under an exclusive source constraint: hard-enforce (drop ineligible works from
  the body entirely) vs enforce-and-disclose (admit flagged exceptions with role disclosure, Dalpha/Bodhi-style)?
  What corpus-shrink is acceptable before Wave 3 must stop (RC-2)? Dalpha proves full compliance is achievable at
  4k words; our corpus may not support it on every task.
- **OQ-4 — The licensed-inference class.** Explicit sign-off that the paragraph-closing inference sentence
  (zero-new-factual-token, union-of-markers) is an approved amendment to the closed-world writer contract —
  it is the single doctrine change in the whole plan, and it sits adjacent to two standing hard rules
  (no post-gen fixes, no entailment machinery). R2.3 is the enforcement mechanism.
- **OQ-5 — Measurement budget.** Approve ≥3v3 paired draws per wave (5 waves + 1 assembled-ladder probe ≈ 33–40
  full generations + judge runs) and the wall-clock envelope per generation.
- **OQ-6 — U10-lite pull-forward.** Whether to move the closing-role resolvability directive (deterministic gate
  0/4→4/4, near-zero cost) from Wave 4 into Wave 1's writer-contract payload.
- **OQ-7 — Deepening in the champion path.** U5 turns the retrieval loop ON at the compose seam
  (`requires_retrieval_pipeline`); confirm the runtime budget permits it in production runs, or whether deepening
  runs only in a corpus-build stage before compose.
- **OQ-8 — FACT track priority.** E.Cit is an orthogonal leaderboard column (champion 165.34 vs our 11). Confirm
  U14 stays coupled to Wave 3 (recommended: it is nearly free there) vs deferred to a separate track.

---

# CLOSING SUMMARY (the three required items)

**Recommended build order:** **Wave 1 (U1 + U12-lite + U14b) → Wave 2 (U3 + U4 + U6) → Wave 3 (U5 + U11 + U14a)
→ Wave 4 (U2 + U8 + U10) → Wave 5 (U7-full + U9 + U13 + U12-full)**, ladder-measured per R5.3, with the critical
path U3 → U4/U6 → U7-full and the assembled-ladder probe closing the initiative.

**First-ship wave + go/no-go:** **Wave 1** — the AC carrier + licensed paragraph-closing inference (U1) +
layout normalizer/preamble removal/single-Limitations (U12-lite) + one-fact-one-cited-sentence (U14b).
GO requires ALL deterministic gates: `paragraph_deduction_rate` 0.16→>0.6; zero-new-factual-token canary = 0
violations; render lint (run-in headings ≥2→0, repeated subheads 4–6→0, preamble absent, verbatim dupes ≥2→0);
normalizer token-multiset equality; AC payload visible in `_compose_section_per_basket` inputs; held-out
deterministic pass on the OQ-1 tasks. THEN a 3v3 paired probe vs the pinned mf_baseline champion (0.5009):
ship iff Insight paired mean > +0.027 (or Insight ≥ 0 with Read > +0.027) and no dimension regresses beyond
−0.027. Cheapest pre-60-min proof: 2–3 sections composed from frozen baskets through the active producer plus
the free render lint on existing artifacts.

**Operator decisions blocking the build:** OQ-1 (held-out set), OQ-2 (lever retirements), OQ-3 (eligibility
posture + acceptable corpus shrink), OQ-4 (licensed-inference doctrine sign-off), OQ-5 (probe budget), OQ-6
(U10-lite pull-forward), OQ-7 (deepening runtime), OQ-8 (FACT coupling). OQ-1, OQ-4, and OQ-5 block Wave 1;
OQ-2 blocks Wave 2; OQ-3 and OQ-7 block Wave 3; the rest can be decided in flight.

## 0. Scope and method

file:line. The build list is exactly the fourteen unified gaps U1–U14 (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:79-664`), the wiring
facts of `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:57-76`, the flat-lever forensics of `:668-679`, and the Phase-4 architectural
thesis both investigators converged on independently (`:810-820`; `phase3_sol_verdict.md:267`;
`phase3_fable_verdict.md:220`). Measured ground truth used throughout: champion = **mf_baseline** (all levers OFF
except `PG_RENDER_BLOCKS=1`, strict-verify bypassed) at Overall mean **0.5009**, draw spread 0.4922–0.5088
(`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:43-55`; `phase3_fable_verdict.md:36-42`); noise bar **±0.027** per
`SCORING_SPEC.md:245`; the two 0.0800 Insight cells are the largest scorecard cells (`SCORING_SPEC.md I.7:121`).

---

# DELIVERABLE A — EXECUTION_CHARTER

Five gating rules. Every fix, in every wave, must pass ALL FIVE before it ships. Each rule states (i) the
enforceable rule, (ii) how a reviewer AUDITS it mechanically, (iii) the stop condition.

## R1 — GENERALIZATION GATE (no task-72 overfit ships)

**Rule.** A fix is admissible only if ALL of the following hold:

1. **Zero task/domain literals** in code, prompts, and config it introduces. Banned classes: task-72 domain nouns
   (e.g. labor/industry/4IR/journal-name strings), benchmark-task identifiers, venue whitelists, and any
   domain-vocabulary lookup table. The only FIXED vocabularies permitted are the audited **role/type/status
   vocabularies**, which are properties of research questions and evidence, not domains
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:306-316`): analytical roles {mechanism, cross-context comparison,
   consensus/disagreement, implications}; query types {factual, causal, comparative, critical}; comparability
   boundaries {population, method, level, period, measure} (the same fields
   `relation_evidence_packs._attributes` already aliases, `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:210-212`); the three-level
   epistemic-status vocabulary (`:381-389`). CONTENT always comes from the question + admitted evidence at run
   time.
2. **Zero magic counts** (no hardcoded section counts, industry counts, word targets) and **zero adjective flag
   names** (no `PG_BETTER_X`-style levers; components are named by the seam/object they build).
3. **Held-out proof.** The fix's deterministic small test (the audit's SMALL TEST for that gap) passes on
   task 72 AND on **≥2 unrelated held-out benchmark tasks** with materially different weight profiles.
   Named candidates from the frozen criteria distribution (`SCORING_SPEC.md I.5:74-77`):
   - **task 91** (Saint Seiya inventory; weights .37 Comp / **.11 Insight** / .32 Inst / .20 Read) — the
     anti-analytical extreme; conditional analytical triggers (U2 mechanism role, U5 analytical-role ledger rows)
     must NOT fire or must degrade gracefully;
   - **task 100** (AI & interpersonal relationships; .29/.40/.16/.15) — the high-Insight analytical extreme;
   - **task 73** (novice EFL teachers; Read **.25**, highest in corpus) — audience/readability stressor;
   - **task 51** (Japan elderly consumption; data-heavy, heading-sparse reference) — data-presentation stressor;
   - **task 4** (zh, gold + mind-map deliverable; .20/.38/.26/.16) — language + explicit format-instruction
     stressor.
   Mandatory pair: **91 + 100** (opposite Insight extremes). Recommended add: 73 or 51. Operator picks the final
   set (Open Question OQ-1).

**Audit procedure.** (a) `grep` the diff for the banned classes: any token that appears in the task-72 prompt or
criteria but not in the fixed role/type/status vocabularies fails review; reviewer greps for digits in prompt/
config additions to catch magic counts. (b) Run the fix's deterministic test on the held-out tasks' frozen
corpora/outlines; the trigger-conditionality check on task 91 is mandatory for any analytically-conditioned
component. (c) Confirm the component reads its content from the constraint extractor
(`compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:398-410`) or admitted evidence rows, never from its own literals.

**Stop.** Any literal/magic-count/adjective-name finding, or a held-out deterministic-test failure, blocks the
wave until fixed. No exceptions for "temporary" literals.

## R2 — FAITHFULNESS FIREWALL (untouchable set + licensed inference + no post-gen edits)

**Rule.**
1. **Untouchable files.** No fix modifies `provenance_generator.py` (rewrite + citation resolution,
   `:3714-3943`, `:4637-5252`) or `clinical_generator/strict_verify.py` (identifier/span/numeric/overlap checks,
   `:387-574`). These are claim-admission controls, not score planners (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:69-73`). No new
   entailment/NLI machinery is introduced anywhere (standing operating rule; the champion runs strict-verify OFF
   and the initiative's fixes must be valid under both strict-verify configurations).
2. **Every sentence still carries evidence markers.** All new writer obligations produce sentences that carry
   `[ev_XXX]` markers exactly as today (`multi_section_generator.py:3517-3519`).
3. **Licensed-inference admission check (the U1 canary).** The ONE new sentence class — the paragraph-closing
   inference — is admitted only under the deterministic **zero-new-factual-token canary**: the closing sentence
   introduces no numeric token, no new proper-noun/entity, and no factual claim absent from the preceding cited
   sentences of that same paragraph, and carries the union of that paragraph's markers
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:111-128`). The same canary gates U7's labeled propositions (each grounded in ≥2 cited
   findings; the label explicitly marks the report's own inference as inference — the faithful way to be novel,
   `:381-389`) and U10's implication objects (derived only from upstream cited propositions, `:469-476`).
4. **NO post-generation content edit, EVER.** No sentence is added, removed, or reworded after composition.
   The NVIDIA-style rubric rewrite is the one competitor move structurally forbidden to us
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:727-729`); its surfaces are absorbed pre-generation into U1/U2/U4/U7/U10.
   **Sole labeled exception — layout-only render normalization**: deterministic markdown layout operations on
   writer-emitted text with ZERO word changes and ZERO content drops (split run-in heading lines at the
   heading/body boundary, enforce blank lines around headings), the same category as the shipping
   `_materialize_paragraph_breaks` (`multi_section_generator.py:3686-3692`) and `markdown_table_normalizer`
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:578-583`). Any operation that touches a word, a number, a marker, or sentence
   membership is a content edit and is banned. The normalizer must ship with a diff-auditor asserting
   token-multiset equality before/after (layout characters excluded).

**Audit procedure.** (a) `git diff --stat` of the wave must show zero lines changed in the untouchable files.
(b) The canary runs as a deterministic lint on every composed artifact (it is Wave 1's shipped test).
(c) The render normalizer's token-multiset equality check runs on every assembly.
(d) Reviewer greps new prompts for instructions that would license out-of-evidence content beyond the one
inference class.

**Stop.** Any untouchable-file diff, any canary failure, any normalizer token change → the wave does not ship.
If strict verification removes a planned sentence, the lawful response is stronger evidence ownership or
regeneration from the plan, never weakening faithfulness or editing the finished report
(`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:724-726`).

## R3 — MEASUREMENT GATE (deterministic first, paired probe second, stop rule)

**Rule.** Two-tier proof, in order, per `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:802-806`:
1. **Tier 1 — deterministic structural assertion FIRST.** Every component ships its audit-named SMALL TEST as a
   zero/low-LLM deterministic assertion (free, noise-immune). A component whose deterministic test fails does not
   proceed to any RACE spend. Concrete gates per wave in §B3.
2. **Tier 2 — paired same-judge multi-draw RACE probe.** ≥3 candidate draws vs ≥3 champion draws (**3v3
   minimum**), same corpus, same judge, otherwise-identical config, decided on the **paired MEAN per-dimension
   delta clearing ±0.027** (the measured baseline spread 0.4922–0.5088 IS this bar,
   `phase3_fable_verdict.md:40`). Never a single-draw comparison.
3. **Build-all-then-measure.** Deterministic tests gate each component individually; the RACE probe measures the
   assembled wave (and, at the end, the assembled ladder), per the standing build-all-then-measure order and
   `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:805-806`. Within-wave components are not RACE-probed one at a time.
4. **STOP RULE.** A component whose deterministic test fails does not ship. A wave whose paired probe shows any
   dimension's paired mean regressing beyond −0.027 does not ship as-is: the wave is decomposed (components
   staged off via the contract's component switches, §R4), the offending component identified by its
   deterministic telemetry, and either repaired or retired. A wave that is flat (all paired means inside ±0.027,
   none negative beyond it) MAY ship only if its deterministic gates all pass AND it is a declared dependency of
   a later wave (foundation shipping), with the flat result recorded — never spun as a win.

**Audit procedure.** Probe manifests record judge model, corpus hash, config hash, draw IDs; reviewer recomputes
the paired means from `results/race/*/raw_results.jsonl`. Any probe missing its paired champion arm is invalid.

**Stop.** As in rule 4 above; additionally, any probe run against a non-champion comparator is void (see R5).

## R4 — THE SHARED-CONTRACT RULE (one contract, consumed by the active producer, audited semantically)

**Rule.** Both Phase-3 investigators converged independently (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:24-28`, `:810-820`): the fix
is **ONE pre-generation analytical contract** — hereafter the **Analytical Contract (AC)** — not a set of
independent flags. Therefore:
1. **One object.** The AC is a single per-run artifact holding the components the waves build: coverage-dimension
   ledger (U5), mechanism/causal-chain spine (U2), divergence ledger + relation graph (U3), comparison groups /
   table plans (U4/U6), epistemically-labeled inference plans (U7), concept-role spine (U8), measurement/effect
   ontology (U9), implication objects (U10), source-admission ledger (U11), block/layout plan (U12), routing
   ownership (U13), external-citation claim contract (U14).
2. **One consumption point.** Every component is consumed by the **active producer,
   `_compose_section_per_basket`** — the verified-compose primary branch — not only by `_call_section`, which is
   the `else` branch (`multi_section_generator.py:6630-6728`; `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:58-61`). A prompt addendum
   routed only to `_call_section` misses the producer that emits the sentences; this exact miswiring is a proven
   cause of the measured-flat lever set (`:668-679`). Definition of done for every wave includes a wiring
   assertion that the component's payload is observable in the active producer's inputs.
3. **Semantic audit.** Fulfillment of any AC obligation is SEMANTIC: the planned/emitted proposition must ENTAIL
   the obligation (dimension-role pair, channel reference, boundary term, label, policy clause), never "the bound
   section is non-empty" — the toothless-audit failure of `coverage_obligations.py:139-158`
   (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:277-283`). ("Semantic" here means checked at proposition level by the audit's
   deterministic tests and fixtures — NOT an NLI/entailment model; R2 bans those.)
4. **No independent flags.** New behavior enters as an AC component with a component switch used ONLY for staged
   measurement (R3's decomposition) and rollback; the switch namespace is the AC's, not a new `PG_*` adjective
   flag. The independent-flag path is what measured flat (`:24-28`, `:818`).
5. **Advisory prose is not a fix.** A wave that only adds writer-prompt sentences without changing evidence
   ownership or plan objects must justify why it will not repeat the measured-flat failure — U1 qualifies because
   it changes the writer's licensed sentence CLASS and ships a deterministic behavioral gate, not because it is
   prose.

**Audit procedure.** (a) Wiring assertion: dump the active producer's assembled inputs on a fixture run and
assert the component payload is present (Sol's U2 test pattern, `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:164-170`). (b) Grep for
new top-level env flags outside the AC namespace. (c) Each obligation's fulfillment test is proposition-level.

**Stop.** A component not consumed by `_compose_section_per_basket`, or audited only by section-nonemptiness,
does not ship.

## R5 — NO-REGRESSION GATE (the champion is the only comparator, and it is protected)

**Rule.**
1. **The comparator is mf_baseline** — all levers OFF except `PG_RENDER_BLOCKS=1`, strict-verify bypassed as
   measured — at Overall mean **0.5009** (draws 0.5088/0.5017/0.4922; `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:46`, `:63-68`;
   `phase3_fable_verdict.md:42`). Every wave's Tier-2 probe runs candidate-vs-champion, paired, same judge, same
   corpus. Measuring against `mf_max`, `7phase`, `faithoff_t72`, or any other strawman is void — the champion is
   the HIGHEST measured config; beating a weaker arm proves nothing.
2. **Ship criterion.** A wave ships only if (a) paired mean Overall ≥ champion within noise (no Overall
   regression beyond −0.027), (b) no dimension regresses beyond −0.027 paired mean, and (c) its claimed win —
   if a win is claimed — clears +0.027 on the targeted dimension(s).
3. **Champion protection.** (i) The champion config (`resolved_lever_states` snapshot,
   `outputs/race_max_focus/mf_baseline-20260723T152731Z/draw_3/compose_summary.json`) is pinned in VCS as a named
   frozen config; (ii) its three baseline draws and their scores are retained as the reference distribution;
   (iii) the champion path stays runnable at all times — AC components default OFF until their wave ships, so
   the shipped pipeline with an empty AC must reproduce the champion behavior byte-for-byte at the config level;
   (iv) after each shipped wave, the new assembled config becomes the NEW champion for the next wave's probes
   (ladder discipline), with its own ≥3-draw reference set.
4. **Corpus/judge pinning.** Probes use the frozen corpus and record the judge model; cross-judge comparisons are
   forbidden (`SCORING_SPEC.md:219-234`: regimes not comparable).

**Audit procedure.** Probe manifest must name the champion config hash and cite its reference draws; an
empty-AC run is diffed against the pinned champion config before any wave's probe is accepted.

**Stop.** Any probe without the champion arm, or any shipped wave that cannot reproduce the champion under
empty-AC, is reverted.

---

# DELIVERABLE B — MASTER_ACTION_PLAN

## B1. BUILD WAVES (grouped by shared seam + dependency; each wave = one coherent contract extension)

Legend: gaps landed · shared AC object built/extended · exact files:functions (from the audit's evidence) ·
pre-gen seam · coherence rationale. All waves obey R1–R5.

### WAVE 1 — "Writer contract + render floor" (U1 + U12-lite + U14b) — the first-ship wave
- **Gaps landed:** U1 (licensed paragraph-closing inference — Insight #7+#8, 0.0800+0.0800); U12-lite (layout
  normalizer, hardcoded-preamble removal, single Limitations role — Read #19/#20/#21/#22/#24, 0.0840 combined,
  our BELOW-parity dim); U14b (one-fact-one-cited-sentence converse rule — FACT #49, rides free on the same
  prompt seam).
- **AC object built:** the **AC carrier itself** (empty-safe, champion-preserving) + its single consumption
  point inside `_compose_section_per_basket`, with the licensed-inference sentence class and the render/layout
  plan as its first two payloads. This satisfies R4 from day one: later waves extend this object; no wave adds a
  flag.
- **Files:functions:** `multi_section_generator.py:3517-3519` (SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE CRITICAL RULES —
  add the inference class), `:4420-4432` (retry contract — same), `:6630-6728` (`_compose_section_per_basket` —
  AC consumption), `:3686-3692` (`_materialize_paragraph_breaks` — the class the new normalizer joins),

## B2. DEPENDENCY GRAPH + CRITICAL PATH

```
W1 U1  (writer inference class)  ──────────────┐   [no upstream deps]
W1 U12-lite (layout normalizer)  ──────────────┤   [no upstream deps]
W1 U14b (one-fact-one-sentence)  ──────────────┤
                                               ▼
W2 U3 (divergence ledger/graph) ──► W2 U4 (comparison groups/tables)
        │                                 └──► W2 U6 (regime schema)
        ├────────────────────────────► W5 U7-full (inference planner + labels)
        ├────────────────────────────► W4 U10-full (implication objects)
        └────────────────────────────► U14 dedup-identity planning (W3)
W3 U5 (coverage ledger + deepening) ──► W2/W5 U6 (instances to compare)
        ├────────────────────────────► W3 U14a (E.Cit volume: more admitted works)
        └────────────────────────────► W5 U13 (ownership needs the ledger)
W3 U11 (admission contract) ─────────► W3 U14a (URLs/quality of admitted works)
W4 U2 (mechanism spine) ─────────────► W1 U1's deductions get channels to reuse (enhances, not required)
        └────────────────────────────► W4 U10-full (implications point at diagnosed levers)
W4 U8 (concept-role spine) ── independent of U2 but same seam
W5 U9 (ontology) ── consumes W2 attributes; disciplines U4/U6 comparisons
W5 U12-full (block plan) ── consumes producer path; hardens everything for render
```

Notes: U1 does NOT require U2 — the audit's before-number (deduction rate 0.16 on mf_baseline) and fable5's
0.5131 Insight at 3,071 words prove the per-paragraph deduction pays standalone
(`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:101-105`); U2 later upgrades those deductions from paragraph-local to spine-reusing.
U6 sits across W2/W3: its schema mechanism is W2's, its instance supply is W3's; its full paired probe waits for
both.

**Critical path (score-weighted):** **U3 → U4/U6 → U7-full** — this chain carries Insight #8 (0.0800), Comp #3
(0.0725), and Insight #10 (0.0640), and its head (U3) converts a measured-zero lever into the substrate for
three later components. Secondary path: **U5 → U6/U13/U14a** (Comp breadth + FACT volume). U1 is off-path
(no dependents block on it) — which is exactly why it ships first: maximum expected value, zero blocking risk.

## B3. PER-WAVE GO/NO-GO (deterministic gates → expected dimensions → probe decision; cheapest pre-60-min test)

**Wave 1.**
- Deterministic gates: (a) `paragraph_deduction_rate` on composed sections: 0.16 (mf_baseline_draw_1) → **>0.6**
  (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:122-125`); (b) zero-new-factual-token canary: **0 violations** (R2.3); (c) render lint:
  run-in heading lines ≥2→**0**, repeated identical subheadings 4–6×→**0**, preamble **absent**, verbatim
  duplicate sentences ≥15 words ≥2→**0** (`:586-592`); (d) normalizer token-multiset equality: **exact**;
  (e) wiring assertion: inference directive present in `_compose_section_per_basket` inputs.
- Cheapest pre-60-min test: **compose 2–3 sections from frozen baskets through the active producer** (minutes,
  no retrieval, no full report) and run gates (a)–(e); the render lint runs on existing artifacts for free.
- Expected dimensions: **Insight ↑** (the two 0.0800 cells), **Read ↑** (0.0840 band, below parity → documented
  +0.045/dim-point at-parity leverage, `:591-592`).
- Probe decision: 3v3 paired vs champion; ship if Insight paired mean > +0.027 OR (Insight ≥ 0 AND Read > +0.027),
  AND no dimension < −0.027. The Read guard doubles as the U1-density risk check (§B5 RC-1).

## B4. SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION

**Recommended build order: W1 → W2 → W3 → W4 → W5**, with the ladder discipline of R5.3 (each shipped wave
becomes the next comparator).

**First-ship wave: WAVE 1.** Rationale against the audit's own ranking (`PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:742-750`):
1. U1 is both investigators' independent #1 (proxy 0.04408 per 0.0800 cell, both cells at once); it needs no
   upstream object, its deterministic test is zero-LLM, and the behavior it targets is the single biggest
   winner-vs-midscorer difference on the board AND locally (fable5 0.5131 Insight vs champ 0.3411,
   `:101-105`).
2. **U1 and U3 are the right first PLAYS but not one wave**: they live in different seams (writer contract vs
   relation layer). Coupling them would violate wave coherence and make the paired probe unattributable. U3
   leads Wave 2 immediately after.
3. **No foundational contract wave needs to precede U1** — provided Wave 1's definition-of-done includes creating
   the AC carrier and its consumption point in `_compose_section_per_basket` (R4.1–R4.2). The contract-first
   requirement is satisfied by making the carrier part of Wave 1, with U1 as its first payload. A carrier-only
   "Wave 0" would ship nothing measurable and burn a probe cycle.
4. U12-lite rides along because it is free (deterministic, layout-only), repairs our only below-parity dimension,
   and its lints become the permanent render gate every later wave reuses. It also hedges U1's known Read-density
   risk in the same probe.
5. The smallest high-confidence first win is therefore exactly this wave: two 0.0800 cells + the 0.0840 Read
   band, gated by five deterministic asserts, provable on frozen baskets in minutes before any 60-minute
   generation.

Weight×headroom justification for the middle order: W2 carries #8+#5+#22-24 and converts a measured-zero lever
(cheapest evidence-to-value conversion in the plan); W3 is the only wave that changes CORPUS breadth (Comp
headroom .49→.55+ is the largest dimension-level headroom, `:323`) and unlocks U6/U13/U14; W4's outline roles
want the post-deepening corpus; W5 governs everything and closes #10.

## B5. RISK REGISTER

**A. Not-fixable-pre-generation (live-with items, from `PIPELINE_GAP_AUDIT.md:715-733`):**
1. **Cleaner nondeterminism/truncation** — judged text is an LLM rewrite we don't control. Plan posture: make
   artifacts robust-to-cleaning (U4's simulated-clean lint, U12's layout hygiene); accept residual risk;
   never claim a cleaning-dependent win from one draw.
2. **Single-call judge variance ±0.027** — irreducible; R3's two-tier design exists because of it; all decisions
   on paired means.
3. **Reference frame + contamination** — RACE is relative to the frozen Gemini reference; not a lever beyond
   adopting the winning surfaces; never compare across judge regimes.
4. **FACT URL reachability / Jina drift** — E.Cit judged against TODAY's pages; posture: canonical stable URLs
   at retrieval (U14 preflight), accept the rest.
5. **Strict-verify sentence removal** — lawful response is stronger evidence ownership or plan-regeneration,
   never firewall weakening (R2).
6. **Post-gen rewrite forbidden** — the NVIDIA move stays absorbed into pre-gen rules; no exception creep beyond
   R2.4's labeled layout normalization.

**B. Fixes that RISK a different cell (cost cells + mitigations):**
- **RC-1 (U1 → Read #19/#22).** Per-paragraph deductions raise analytic density; the #1/#3/#4 leaderboard systems
  all bottom out on Read (cellcog 51.94, Bodhi 51.87, Lunon 50.48 — addendum F2, `COMPETITOR_TEARDOWN.md:399-401`).
  We start BELOW parity so we cannot afford the winners' trade. Mitigation: the rule licenses exactly ONE closing
  inference sentence per paragraph (structural cap, not a count knob); U12-lite ships in the same wave; Wave 1's
  probe reads Read as a co-primary guard.
- **RC-2 (U11 → Comp #2/#3/#5 and FACT E.Cit).** Eligibility enforcement shrinks the corpus (tier compliance
  ceiling ~73% measured, `:495-497`); Sourcery proves a thin sector base forfeits ~3 Comp points
  (`:293-295`). Mitigation: U5's typed deepening refills to the ledger's satisfaction BEFORE enforcement
  verdicts are final; unknown-eligibility rows become retrieval targets, not silent drops; Wave 3's gates couple
  ledger fulfillment WITH compliance so neither ships alone; residual shortfall is disclosed Dalpha/Bodhi-style
  (the honest scope note itself performs the constraint, `COMPETITOR_TEARDOWN.md:71-73`). Operator decision OQ-3
  governs the hard-enforce vs enforce-and-disclose balance.
- **RC-3 (U5 deepening → Inst #13 focus + Read walls).** More admitted evidence historically became more prose
  (route-all, 433-ID residual dump, `:599-604`). Mitigation: W3 ships ledger-SCOPED admission only; U13's
  marginal-contribution routing lands in W5 — until then, W3's gate includes "no residual-section role" as a
  regression lint (it is free) so the dump cannot silently return.
- **RC-4 (U4 tables → Read #23/#24 under the cleaner).** Any reintroduced table can degrade in-flight
  (`:717-719`). Mitigation: tables only from table PLANS with writer-emitted short-phrase cells under the
  existing rule (`:3595-3600`), always followed by the interpretation paragraph; the simulated-clean lint is a
  permanent gate; where comparability fails, plan prose (fable5 won Read 0.5262 WITH 14 table rows — well-formed
  tables are not the enemy, malformed ones are).
- **RC-5 (U7 labels → fabrication-adjacent risk).** A "this review's own hypothesis" label on an ungrounded claim
  would be unfaithful novelty. Mitigation: labels REQUIRE ≥2 cited premises + the R2.3 canary; the confounded
  fixture must yield "unresolved", not a hypothesis; unlabeled novelty remains banned.
- **RC-6 (U2 on non-analytical tasks → Inst/Comp).** A mechanism section on an inventory task (t91, Insight .11)
  would misallocate real estate. Mitigation: question-type conditionality via the constraint extractor + the
  mandatory t91 held-out no-fire test (R1.3).
- **RC-7 (U14 atomic cited sentences → Read #22 serial-summary).** One-fact-one-sentence can drift toward
  staccato inventory. Mitigation: the rule applies only to independently supported DISTINCT facts; paragraph
  organization stays claim-led (U1's closing inference gives each paragraph its synthesis); Read guard in every
  probe.
- **RC-8 (probe budget/schedule).** 5 waves × ≥3v3 draws ≈ 30+ full generations + judge calls; under-budgeting
  invites single-draw shortcuts, which R3 forbids. Mitigation: OQ-5 fixes the budget up front; deterministic
  gates kill weak components before any RACE spend.

## B6. OPEN QUESTIONS FOR THE OPERATOR (decisions blocking the build)

- **OQ-1 — Held-out set.** Confirm the generalization pair {task 91, task 100} + one of {73, 51, 4} (R1.3), and
  whether held-out runs are outline/compose-level only (cheap) or full-report (costly, more faithful).
- **OQ-2 — Retirements.** Confirm permanent retirement of `PG_SYNTHESIS_TABLE_CONSTRUCT` /
  `PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_COMPOSE` (wrong idea + post-gen violation) and the raw relation-pack JSON dump, vs
  keep-behind-AC-switch for forensics.
- **OQ-3 — Eligibility posture.** Under an exclusive source constraint: hard-enforce (drop ineligible works from
  the body entirely) vs enforce-and-disclose (admit flagged exceptions with role disclosure, Dalpha/Bodhi-style)?
  What corpus-shrink is acceptable before Wave 3 must stop (RC-2)? Dalpha proves full compliance is achievable at
  4k words; our corpus may not support it on every task.
- **OQ-4 — The licensed-inference class.** Explicit sign-off that the paragraph-closing inference sentence
  (zero-new-factual-token, union-of-markers) is an approved amendment to the closed-world writer contract —
  it is the single doctrine change in the whole plan, and it sits adjacent to two standing hard rules
  (no post-gen fixes, no entailment machinery). R2.3 is the enforcement mechanism.
- **OQ-5 — Measurement budget.** Approve ≥3v3 paired draws per wave (5 waves + 1 assembled-ladder probe ≈ 33–40
  full generations + judge runs) and the wall-clock envelope per generation.
- **OQ-6 — U10-lite pull-forward.** Whether to move the closing-role resolvability directive (deterministic gate
  0/4→4/4, near-zero cost) from Wave 4 into Wave 1's writer-contract payload.
- **OQ-7 — Deepening in the champion path.** U5 turns the retrieval loop ON at the compose seam
  (`requires_retrieval_pipeline`); confirm the runtime budget permits it in production runs, or whether deepening
  runs only in a corpus-build stage before compose.
- **OQ-8 — FACT track priority.** E.Cit is an orthogonal leaderboard column (champion 165.34 vs our 11). Confirm
  U14 stays coupled to Wave 3 (recommended: it is nearly free there) vs deferred to a separate track.

---

# CLOSING SUMMARY (the three required items)

**Recommended build order:** **Wave 1 (U1 + U12-lite + U14b) → Wave 2 (U3 + U4 + U6) → Wave 3 (U5 + U11 + U14a)
→ Wave 4 (U2 + U8 + U10) → Wave 5 (U7-full + U9 + U13 + U12-full)**, ladder-measured per R5.3, with the critical
path U3 → U4/U6 → U7-full and the assembled-ladder probe closing the initiative.

**First-ship wave + go/no-go:** **Wave 1** — the AC carrier + licensed paragraph-closing inference (U1) +
layout normalizer/preamble removal/single-Limitations (U12-lite) + one-fact-one-cited-sentence (U14b).
GO requires ALL deterministic gates: `paragraph_deduction_rate` 0.16→>0.6; zero-new-factual-token canary = 0
violations; render lint (run-in headings ≥2→0, repeated subheads 4–6→0, preamble absent, verbatim dupes ≥2→0);
normalizer token-multiset equality; AC payload visible in `_compose_section_per_basket` inputs; held-out
deterministic pass on the OQ-1 tasks. THEN a 3v3 paired probe vs the pinned mf_baseline champion (0.5009):
ship iff Insight paired mean > +0.027 (or Insight ≥ 0 with Read > +0.027) and no dimension regresses beyond
−0.027. Cheapest pre-60-min proof: 2–3 sections composed from frozen baskets through the active producer plus
the free render lint on existing artifacts.

**Operator decisions blocking the build:** OQ-1 (held-out set), OQ-2 (lever retirements), OQ-3 (eligibility
posture + acceptable corpus shrink), OQ-4 (licensed-inference doctrine sign-off), OQ-5 (probe budget), OQ-6
(U10-lite pull-forward), OQ-7 (deepening runtime), OQ-8 (FACT coupling). OQ-1, OQ-4, and OQ-5 block Wave 1;
OQ-2 blocks Wave 2; OQ-3 and OQ-7 block Wave 3; the rest can be decided in flight.
